evals.prompt: Test whether the framed intent's bet holds before building it out.
evals.expected_output: A de-risk pass that triages reversibility (one-way vs two-way door), names a single riskiest assumption as 'what would have to be true', predeclares a kill condition in the test's own currency BEFORE any result (a number where there is traffic, a qualitative bar where there isn't), chooses a prototype-approach (validate-first vs prototype-led) consistent with the triage, and ends in a survive/kill verdict compared against the predeclared line. It stays at de-risking — it does not re-author or decompose the intent.
assertions: Triages reversibility — classifies the bet as a one-way or two-way door
Names a single riskiest assumption, fronted as 'what would have to be true'
Predeclares a kill condition in the test's own currency, stated before the result (number where there's traffic, qualitative bar where there isn't)
Chooses a prototype-approach (validate-first or prototype-led) consistent with the reversibility triage
Ends in an explicit survive/kill verdict compared against the predeclared line
Stays at de-risking — does not author (frame) or break down (decompose) the intent
